etymology.description: semantic 其(其) (qí, "sieve; basket") + phonetic 斤(斤) (jīn, "axe") -> the original meaning "to divide/break with an axe" -> borrowed for phonetic value to mean "this"
etymology.interpretation: The semantic component 其(其) (qí, "sieve; basket") implies separation or sorting, which associates well with the idea of dividing with an axe, represented by 斤(斤) (jīn, "axe") as the phonetic component. The character was used for its phonetic value to indicate "this."

examples.chinese: 在斯地(在斯地)，我找到了平静(我找到了平靜)。
examples.pinyin: Zài sī dì, wǒ zhǎodào le píngjìng.
examples.english: In this place, I found peace.

examples.chinese: 此乃斯人(此乃斯人)，吾心之所向(吾心之所向)。
examples.pinyin: Cǐ nǎi sī rén, wú xīn zhī suǒ xiàng.
examples.english: This is the person whom my heart desires.

examples.chinese: 斯书曾伴我多年(斯書曾伴我多年)，如今倍感珍惜(如今倍感珍惜)。
examples.pinyin: Sī shū céng bàn wǒ duō nián, rújīn bèi gǎn zhēnxī.
examples.english: This book has accompanied me for many years, and now I treasure it even more.
